title: calea teucriifolia
intro: Calea teucriifolia, also known as Mexican Damiana or Mexican Heather, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. Calea teucriifolia is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving anxiety and insomnia, lowering blood pressure, and treating gastrointestinal issues. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote relaxation and well-being.
